safest_plan.entry_basis: zero_live 2026-06-05 — NOT a live limit. Wait for price to sweep the low near $71 then reclaim and accept above it; the buy zone is the $72 0.618 demand (labelled '$72' on the 09-NFLX chart), which sits just BELOW the prior ~$76 stop. The shallower $78 level (5D gap-fill 'minimum' from the chart annotation) is the first place price stalls, but the preferred entry is the deeper $71–72 reclaim.

targets.basis: zero_live 2026-06-05 grade — lower bound of the '82–84+' target zone (back into prior value)
zero_live 2026-06-05 grade — '82–84+' target zone; chart green-arrow long projection off the $72 demand

safest_plan.stop_basis: A daily close below the swept low near $71 and below the $72 0.618 demand zone. Also macro-invalid if the S&P 500 breaks down hard (zero_live 2026-06-05) — at all-time highs the regime risk is elevated.

stance_by_source.summary: Down move has played out to ~82; expects further rebalance to $78 minimum (5D gap fill) with deeper demand at $71–72. Plan is to WAIT, then buy a sweep-and-reclaim of ~$71 for a long to 82–84+ — but only if the S&P 500 doesn't break down hard. Self-graded weak (4).

Failed-breakout (IVB) short: needs price to spend time below the $100 key level (~99.3 breaker), then short on a retest, targeting the 72–78 gap-fill zone. Invalid on a close back above $100 with acceptance. Self-graded partial (5).

conflicts: The long is conditional, not a clean buy: Zero requires price to first dip to ~$71, sweep the lows, then reclaim AND hold — and the whole idea only holds if the S&P 500 doesn't break down hard while it sits at record highs.

Stance evolved rather than agreed cleanly: the older 27-Apr read was a SHORT down into the 72–78 zone; the newer 5-Jun read treats that same 71–78 zone as a place to BUY. The 5-Jun (newer) view governs — short thesis has largely played out, now it's wait-to-buy at the target.
plain_english: Both of Zero's recent reads point the same way: Netflix is expected to fall further first — filling a price gap down to around $78, with a deeper buy area near $71–72. He is not buying now. The plan is to wait for price to dip to roughly $71, briefly spike below to grab the lows, then climb back and hold above that level — that reclaim would be the signal to go long toward $82–84. Even then it only makes sense if the wider US market (the S&P 500) doesn't crash, because the S&P is at record highs and adds risk. So do nothing yet: wait for price to reach ~$71–72 and confirm it reclaims before considering a buy.
